one_time_description: Treaty loan forgiveness - $5,614,886 in comprehensive land claim negotiation debt owed to Canada was forgiven effective March 31, 2020
economic_notes: Nation has significant business enterprises including seafoods, resort (Tin Wis Resort), and micro hydro power operations (Haa-ak-suuk, Winchie, Canoe Creek). COVID-19 noted as significant event in March 2020 with unknown future impact.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2020. ISC funding flows through Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council (NTC). Total government transfers include NTC ($5,445,782), Province of BC ($376,115), CMHC ($232,118), DFO ($130,809), FNHA ($30,253), BCTC ($121,233) = $6,336,310 identifiable; BC Gaming ($732,437) may also be a government transfer bringing total to ~$6,968,747. Major one-time item: $5,614,886 treaty loan forgiveness. Nation has complex structure with multiple GBEs (Economic Development Corp, TFN Master LP, Tsiix-Waa-Cahs Holdings) accounted for by modified equity method. Business enterprises include micro-hydro power, seafood operations, and Tin Wis Resort with significant long-term debt (~$49M at entity level). Net debt position of ($11,259,484). COVID-19 noted as subsequent event. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. expenses_infrastructure mapped to Public Works segment.
